description: put all the items in the tab bar like the others and actually you can put some on the right side as well kind of mirror the TikTok UI and now users it shouldn't be like that it should be a particular account my account and I should be able to sign in and out of it and stuff like that

results.summary: Rebuilt the feed with a TikTok-style right-side action rail and turned the Users tab into a single 'my account' Profile with sign in/out
results.identified_issue: The voice note is a two-part TikTok redesign: (1) move the feed's controls into a TikTok layout ('put some on the right side, mirror the TikTok UI'), and (2) make the third tab a single personal account with sign in/out instead of a user roster. I read (1) as TikTok's vertical action column, so the feed's bottom toolbar became a right-edge rail; the bottom tab bar stays as the section nav (that already IS TikTok's bottom bar). For (2) the app has NO auth backend (TokenKeychain is only the OpenRouter key), and the multi-user roster is load-bearing for the one-answer-per-side-per-person rule — so I did NOT rip it out. I made isSignedIn a local flag on UserStore and kept the roster as a secondary 'Switch account' section. Real authentication (Sign in with Apple / a server) and whether to drop multi-user entirely are product calls left for the human. Alternative rejected: gating the whole app behind sign-in — too entangled and TikTok lets you browse signed-out, so sign-in is scoped to the Profile tab only.
results.detailed_description: I took the voice note as two asks and did both. First, the feed now mirrors TikTok: the controls that used to sit in a bottom toolbar (answer, browse questions, show/hide the question, settings) are now a vertical action rail on the right edge of the video, stacked as circular glyphs with the 'Answer' button tinted as the primary action. The bottom tab bar keeps its three sections — that's already TikTok's bottom bar. Second, the 'Users' tab is now a 'Profile' tab that leads with a single account: your avatar, name, answer count, an Edit-name chip, and Sign out. Signing out drops the tab to a Sign-in screen (name + Sign in) and nothing is deleted; signing back in restores it. Because the same phone can still hold several people's takes (the one-answer-per-side-per-person rule depends on it), the old roster lives on as a 'Switch account' section lower down, matching how a real profile carries an account switcher. Heads-up on two things: (a) I could NOT build or run this — the testables flow forbids building, and vigil builds/deploys from the pushed commit, so please check it on device. The SourceKit 'cannot find type' warnings I saw are false positives from not having the whole module compiled. (b) Sign in/out is a LOCAL flag right now — the app has no auth backend. If you want real accounts, the two decisions I need are: which identity provider (Sign in with Apple is the no-backend option; anything cross-device needs a server), and whether the multi-person roster should be removed entirely or kept as the switcher I left in. Also tell me if by 'put all the items in the tab bar' you meant something other than the right-side rail and I'll adjust.
